description: This figure is a schematic of true stress (unit: MPa) versus true strain curves illustrating flow stress evolution of low-alloy steel under varying hot deformation conditions, where Zener-Hollomon parameter Zp (with Zp2>Zp1) represents the combined effect of deformation temperature and strain rate. Four curves are included: black dashed line for constant Zp1, purple dashed line for constant Zp2, blue solid line for Zp decreasing from Zp2 to Zp1 at intermediate true strain εl, and red solid line for Zp increasing from Zp1 to Zp2 at εl. The process is divided into two stages: Stage I (before deformation condition variation, up to εl) and Stage II (after variation). Critical strains for dynamic recrystallization follow the order εc1 < εc2 < εl, and higher Zp corresponds to higher peak stress and steady-state stress. Transient Zp adjustment at εl induces an abrupt stress jump, followed by stress evolution to the steady-state level matching the new Zp condition. This schematic demonstrates the flow stress response under varying thermomechanical conditions, supporting investigation of multi-stage dynamic recrystallization behavior of low-alloy steel during hot forming.
